title: Why LLMs Forget
concept: Without constraints, updating weights on a narrow domain dataset pushes the model's parameters away from the general text representations it learned during pretraining.

exercises.prompt: What happens to a general LLM if we train it on a narrow topic without any limitations?

options.text: It becomes smarter at absolutely everything.
It might forget how to do general tasks.
It stops responding to any inputs whatsoever.

exercises.explain: LLMs can lose their previously learned general abilities when we train them on new, specific data without constraints.

cards.title: The Brain Drain
cards.body: Have you ever studied so hard for a history exam that you completely forgot how to do the math formulas you learned last week? Large Language Models do the exact same thing! When they learn new, specialized skills, they often completely wipe out their general knowledge.

cards.title: Catastrophic Forgetting
cards.body: When we fine-tune a model on a small, specific dataset without safeguards, we update its internal weights. Without constraints, these updates push the model's parameters far away from the balanced configurations it learned during broad pretraining. This loss of general knowledge is called 'catastrophic forgetting'.

cards.title: The Pizza Chef Dilemma
cards.body: Imagine you are a world-class chef who knows how to cook hundreds of global dishes. Suddenly, you get a job at a restaurant that only serves gourmet pepperoni pizzas. You make pizza all day, every day. Months later, someone asks you to cook a traditional French soufflé. Because your brain has been entirely rewritten to optimize for pizza, you've forgotten the delicate steps for the soufflé!

cards.title: The Legalese Takeover
cards.body: Let's say you have an LLM that is excellent at writing emails, stories, and code. You decide to fine-tune it on 5,000 legal contracts so it learns to draft legal text. If you don't limit how much the weights can change, the model will excel at contracts but will suddenly start writing casual emails in confusing legalese!

cards.title: Not a Storage Problem
cards.body: People often think models forget because they 'run out of memory' or have too little storage. In reality, the model has plenty of space! It forgets because the mathematical settings (weights) that allowed it to speak generally are actively overwritten by the new, narrow training.

cards.title: Balanced vs. Over-shifted Weights
cards.body: See how focusing too hard on a narrow dataset shifts the model's internal connections away from its original, balanced state.

right.text: Weights heavily shifted to favor only the narrow target data, ruining general capabilities.

left.text: Balanced weights distributed to handle creative writing, math, coding, and general conversation.

exercises.prompt: Why does an LLM lose its general capabilities when fine-tuned on a very narrow dataset?

options.text: The model actively deletes its old training files to save disk space.
Its weights shift away from the general text representations it learned before.
The model's vocabulary list is automatically cut in half.
Computers naturally lose electrical charge over time.

exercises.explain: Without constraints, the model shifts its internal parameters to fit only the new, narrow dataset, losing the general representations it learned during pretraining.

exercises.prompt: True or False: LLMs forget general information because their hard drives physically run out of storage space.
exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting is caused by weight updates shifting parameters away from general representations, not by running out of hardware storage.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence to explain why LLMs lose general skills during fine-tuning.
exercises.sentence: Without constraints, updating a model's ___ on a narrow dataset overwrites its general knowledge.
exercises.explain: Updating a model's weights on a narrow dataset shifts its parameters away from the general representations it learned during pretraining.

exercises.prompt: Identify which response shows signs of catastrophic forgetting after a model is trained on medical records.

options.text: Hello! I can help you write a fun story about a dog, or explain science concepts!
To answer your simple greeting: Patient presents with acute greeting symptoms, prescribe hello.
I cannot answer this request because it violates safety guidelines.

exercises.explain: The AI response shows classic catastrophic forgetting: it has replaced its general conversational skills with the narrow medical jargon it was recently trained on.

exercises.prompt: When we train a general LLM on a very specific set of medical texts without any constraints, why does it lose its ability to write general poetry?

options.text: Its database runs out of storage space to hold old words.
Its weights shift to favor the new task, overwriting the old patterns.
The training data is automatically deleted by the system.
The model's neural network physically shrinks during training.

exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting happens when a model updates its weights too aggressively for a new, narrow task, causing it to overwrite the general knowledge weights it developed during pretraining.
